items.headline: Hungary opposition wins two-thirds majority ending Orbán's 16-year rule
items.body: Centre-right Tisza party secured 138 of 199 parliament seats with turnout exceeding 77 percent. Supermajority allows constitutional amendments and unblocks 90 billion euro loan to Ukraine previously vetoed by Orbán government.

items.headline: United States begins naval blockade of Iran and Strait of Hormuz
items.body: American forces began enforcing port restrictions after talks collapsed and Trump administration deadline passed. Blockade affects route carrying one-fifth of global fuel with Iran warning all Gulf ports at risk.

items.headline: China tells Taiwan opposition leader independence will not be tolerated
items.body: Xi Jinping met visiting Kuomintang chairwoman in first party leadership talks since 2015. KMT has blocked 40 billion dollar defense budget proposed by Taiwan president to counter potential Chinese military action.

items.headline: Uganda president wins seventh term as opposition leader goes into hiding
items.body: Museveni secured 72 percent in vote UN described as marred by repression and internet blackout. Opposition candidate reported security forces surrounded his home before he escaped to undisclosed location after results announced.
